basic_details.job_title: CARI Junior Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Walkin
basic_details.short_information: Central Ayurveda Research Institute (CARI) has released an official notification for Walk-in Interview for 1 Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology Posts. Eligible candidates can attend the Walk-in Interview on at the CARI office with original documents and self-attested copies.

age_limit.details: The maximum age limit for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology post is 30 years as of 01.01.2026. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC candidates will be as per Govt. of India Rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Post Graduate degree in Microbiology/Biotechnology/Life Sciences and relevant subjects, with a minimum of 55% marks.
qualification.details: Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology: Post Graduate degree in Microbiology/Biotechnology/Life Sciences and relevant subjects, with a minimum of 55% marks from a recognized university. Preference will be given to candidates having a Masters degree in Microbiology with experience in Microbiology techniques, including sub-culturing, preservation, antimicrobial assay, anaerobic culture, MIC, and preparation of extracts from plant materials. Priority will be given to GATE/NET/Ph.D. qualified candidates.

salary_details.details: Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology: Rs. 37000 + HRA (20%) per month

selection_process: The selection process for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology post will be based on a Walk-in Interview.

faq.question: Q1. What is the post name for the CARI recruitment?
faq.answer: The post name is Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology. This is a project-based research role at the Central Ayurveda Research Institute.

faq.question: Q2. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy available for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology position. This is a single opening for eligible candidates.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Post Graduate degree in Microbiology, Biotechnology, Life Sciences, or relevant subjects with a minimum of 55% marks from a recognized university.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Junior Research Fellow (JRF) post?
faq.answer: The age limit is not exceeding 30 years as of January 1, 2026. Age relaxation will be provided as per government rules for specific categories.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for this position?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a salary of Rs. 37000 plus HRA (20%) per month. This is a consolidated monthly remuneration.

faq.question: Q6. How can I apply for the CARI Junior Research Fellow (JRF) Microbiology position?
faq.answer: The application process is through a Walk-in Interview. Candidates need to report to the specified venue on the interview date with all required documents.

faq.question: Q7. When and where is the Walk-in Interview scheduled?
faq.answer: The Walk-in Interview will be held on 16th June 2026 at the Central Ayurveda Research Institute, Jhansi, Uttar Pradesh, PIN 284003.

faq.question: Q8. What documents should I carry for the Walk-in Interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, including educational certificates, experience certificates (if any), ID proof, and category certificates, along with self-attested photocopies.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any preference given to certain candidates?
faq.answer: Yes, preference will be given to candidates with a Masters degree in Microbiology and relevant experience. Priority will also be given to GATE/NET/Ph.D. qualified candidates.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification can be accessed through the provided link in the "Important Links" section of this article. It contains all detailed information.
